main_ingredients: Paleta Iberica de Bellota (Iberian shoulder ham)

serving.natural_language_portion: One serving is a whole 12-inch pizza, estimated at approximately 500 g, as typically served at Ricciotti restaurant in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Pizza with meat, regular crust (SR Legacy / Foundation Foods composite); supplemented with USDA entries for Gorgonzola cheese, Mascarpone cheese, cured pork shoulder, pear, and walnuts for ingredient-level adjustment.
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct database entry exists for Iberico and Pear Pizza. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted ingredient composition: pizza dough base (~40%), Gorgonzola DOP (~10%), Mascarpone (~8%), Paleta Iberica de Bellota cured ham (~15%), Packham pear (~12%), roasted walnuts (~8%), and residual sauce/oil (~7%). USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries used for each component. Values represent per-100g of the assembled baked pizza. Energy cross-checked using Atwater factors.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. Pizza, cheese, thin crust (GI ~50, reference glucose).
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Iberico and Pear Pizza. GI of 50 is adopted from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for cheese pizza on regular/thin crust (GI ~50, tested against glucose reference). The Iberico and Pear variant has a similar dough base and fat-rich topping profile, which suppresses glycemic response comparably. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (50 × 122.5) / 100 = 61.3, where carbohydrate per 500g serving = 24.5 × 5 = 122.5g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~520 mg per 100g; ~2600 mg per whole pizza) from cured Iberian ham, Gorgonzola DOP, and Mascarpone
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this pizza due to its very high sodium load from cured meats and aged cheeses; a single whole pizza may exceed the WHO daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (~50) but high glycemic load per serving (~61) due to large refined-dough portion
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this pizza in small portions; while the GI is moderate-low, the high carbohydrate load per full serving will produce a significant postprandial glucose rise and should be paired with careful portion control.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat (~6.2g/100g) from Gorgonzola, Mascarpone, and Iberian ham, combined with high sodium and cholesterol
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should treat this pizza as an occasional indulgence; the combination of saturated fat, dietary cholesterol (~38 mg/100g), and high sodium is unfavourable for LDL cholesterol management and blood pressure control.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Iberico e Pere is a 12-inch Italian-style pizza served at Ricciotti restaurant in Singapore. It is topped with Paleta Iberica de Bellota — a premium Spanish acorn-fed Iberian shoulder ham — Gorgonzola D.O.P (a protected-designation Italian blue cheese), Mascarpone cheese, sliced Packham pear, and roasted walnuts on a baked pizza dough base. The flavour profile is a sophisticated balance of savoury, salty, and umami notes from the cured ham and Gorgonzola, offset by the mild sweetness of the pear and the buttery richness of Mascarpone, with textural contrast from the crunchy walnuts. Nutritionally, this pizza is energy-dense (~272 kcal/100g), moderately high in protein (~11.5g/100g) from the cheese and cured meat, and relatively high in fat (~14.8g/100g) with a notable saturated fat component (~6.2g/100g). Sodium content is high (~520 mg/100g) due to the cured ham and aged cheeses, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 50 (low-moderate), consistent with cheese pizza on regular crust, but the glycemic load per whole pizza is high (~61) due to the large dough portion. This dish is not suitable for halal or kosher diets and contains gluten, dairy, and tree nut (walnut) allergens. It is best consumed as an occasional treat rather than a regular dietary staple.

confidence_reason: No direct database entry exists for this specific restaurant pizza. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component data using weighted ingredient proportions. GI is adopted from the closest analogous food (cheese pizza, thin crust) in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than low because the ingredient list is fully specified and each component has a reliable USDA source anchor.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are ingredient-composition estimates based on USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data; no direct laboratory analysis of this dish is available. 2. Serving size of 500g is an estimate for a whole 12-inch pizza; actual weight may vary by restaurant preparation. 3. Sodium estimate may be conservative; actual sodium from Gorgonzola DOP and Paleta Iberica de Bellota could be higher depending on specific product batches. 4. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are all estimated from USDA component data and are not independently verified for this specific dish; these values are provided as best-available estimates and are not sentinel -1 values. 5. This is an Italian cuisine dish served in Singapore; multilingual naming rules for Singaporean dishes do not apply as this is not a Singaporean dish by origin or cuisine classification. No mandatory Chinese, Malay, Tamil, or Hindi names are required under the Veritas multilingual rules for Italian cuisine. 6. GI glycemic_load_per_serving of 61.3 is calculated for a full 500g pizza serving: (50 × 122.5g carbohydrate) / 100 = 61.3. 7. The cuisine_tag 'Chicken Dish' in the input record appears to be a data artefact from the source platform and has been disregarded as this dish contains no chicken.
